prevalence.source: European hematology epidemiology syntheses (AIHA and hereditary hemolytic disorders)

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; can be significant in severe or chronic cases
burden.mortalityRate: Low to moderate depending on etiology and comorbidity

vulnerablePopulations: patients with autoimmune disease or malignancy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Anemia with hemolysis markers and smear; DAT/Coombs for immune hemolysis; evaluate underlying disease and triggers

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands across the EU (broad category)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Generally good with targeted therapy and specialist care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Comprehensive hematology services and lab diagnostics widely available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Cause-directed care; steroids and rituximab/immunosuppressants for AIHA when indicated; transfusions and supportive care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Managing frail comorbid patients and ensuring rapid evaluation in acute hemolysis
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong specialty hematology care and access to advanced therapies
